title: 3 Biblical Tests to Discern God's Voice
hook: Think you heard God — but not sure? Try these 3 biblical tests before you act.
script: Think you heard God — but not sure? Try these 3 biblical tests before you act. First: Scripture alignment. Place the impression beside Scripture; if it contradicts God's Word, set it aside. Second: peace and fruit over time. True guidance brings steady peace and produces love, patience and wise choices—not panic or selfish gain. Third: community confirmation. Ask mature believers for perspective. Then pray simply, "Lord, give clarity and peace," and take small, patient steps. Discernment grows; uncertainty doesn't mean you're failing.

title: How to Know It's God: 3 Quick Checks
hook: If you're tired of mistaking your thoughts for God's voice, listen to this.
script: If you're tired of mistaking your thoughts for God's voice, listen to this. Check one: the Bible test — does this idea line up with Scripture and God's character? Check two: the peace and fruit test — does it create calm conviction and produce godly fruit over time, or does it spark anxiety and secrecy? Check three: the community test — do trusted Christians confirm or challenge it? Pray, "Lord, lead me by truth and peace," then wait for alignment of Word, peace, and wise counsel.

title: Hear God, Not Your Thoughts: 3 Steps
hook: You need to hear this today: three simple tests to tell God's voice from your thoughts.
script: You need to hear this today: three simple tests to tell God's voice from your thoughts. Imagine two inner voices — one rushed, one calm. Test one: Scripture — does the prompting reflect God's commands and character? Test two: inner peace and fruit — true guidance gives a steady peace and produces good fruit rather than urgency or shame. Test three: community — seek a wise Christian's view. Pray, "Father, help me hear You clearly," wait for matching Word, peace, and counsel, and remember: discernment is learned.
